### Website 179: Emory Hacks
- Card URL: [https://www.emoryhacks.com/](https://www.emoryhacks.com/)
- Referenced by: Emory Hacks
- Rendered title: Emory Hacks
- Final URL: [https://emoryhacks.com/](https://emoryhacks.com/)
- Expanded FAQ/accordion controls: 0

2025 FALL

#### EMORY HACKSEMORY HACKS

11/14 - 11/16

[REGISTRATION CLOSED](https://forms.gle/TV76qB2en6bNJTiM9)

##### ABOUT

Emory Hacks, presented by PROJECT Emory, is a hackathon hosted at Emory University. We are committed to bringing hundreds of students together for an intensive 36-hour hackathon where innovation comes to life. Whether you're a first-time coder or a seasoned developer come bond peers and industry professionals, and join us to push your creative and technical boundaries in this dynamic weekend of building and collaboration.

##### TRACKS

HEALTHCARE

Develop innovative solutions to improve patient care, streamline medical processes, or enhance health outcomes using technology. Projects could include telemedicine platforms, health monitoring apps, medical data analysis tools, or accessibility solution for healthcare.

EDUCATION

Build tools and platforms that transform learning experiences and educational access. Create solutions for online learning, student engagement, educational games, tutoring systems, or tools that help educators and students succeed.

SMART CITY

Design technology solutions for urban challenges and community improvement. Develop apps or systems for transportation, sustainability, public safety, civic engagement, or making city services more efficient and accessible to residents.

ROGUE

Open-ended track for beginners to work on any project they find interesting. No specific theme or requirements, just build something you want to make. Could be a web app, mobile app, game, or any other software project. Focus is on learning and experimenting rather than competing for major prizes. Smaller prize pool compared to main tracks.

##### SCHEDULE

Friday Saturday Sunday

02:00 PM Participant Check-in

03:30 PM Opening Ceremony

05:00 PM Hackathon Begins

06:00 PM Dinner

06:00 PM Vectus Mentors

06:30 PM MLH Google AI Studio

07:30 PM Workshop 1

10:00 PM Late-night Snack

##### SPEAKERS

Raphael Palacio Sarah O'Brien Sridhar Hallyala Zachary Farley Rosalind McCullough

##### FAQ

Is the event free to attend?

Yes, the event is free to attend. The Emory Hacks team will be providing food and swag for all hackers attending the event.

Where is the event? Is it in person or virtual? Where can I park?

The event is located at Emory University’s Goizueta Business School at 1300 Clifton Rd N E, Atlanta, GA 30322. You can park at the Fishburne Parking Deck, Oxford Road Parking Deck, and Gambrell Parking deck, which you can see on this campus parking site - https://transportation.emory.edu/visitor-parking

Who can attend? What if I don’t have any experience? Do I have to be 18?

This event is open to any students. It is beginner friendly, with workshops to help you learn during the event, and mentors available to help you as you work on your project. Attendees must be at least 13 years old due to child privacy laws. If under 18, you can attend, but will need to fill out a liability form from the university.

What is the team size limit?

You can be on teams of up to 4. We will have a team building activity right after the opening ceremony if you need to find team members!

Are there travel reimbursements?

We will not be providing travel reimbursements at this time.

What should I bring?

Please bring your laptop, battery packs, chargers, headphones, ID (Government Issue), blanket, pillow, and personal toiletries.

When can we start working on our project? Can I work on a previous project?

You cannot start until after the opening ceremony. You may come up with ideas, but are not allowed to start coding. You cannot work on a previous project, but can use frameworks if you clearly credit them in your readme and differentiate what you made vs what you used.

Do I have to stay overnight?

No, you can leave and come back if you would prefer to go to bed. Here are some suggestions: University Inn at Emory, DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel Atlanta North Druid Hills, La Quinta Inn & Suites by Wyndham Atlanta Midtown, Holiday Inn Express & Suites Atlanta - Emory University

What kind of activities will there be?

The hackathon will provide workshops, panels with speakers, and much more!

What is a hackathon? Will there be actual hacking?

A hackathon is an event where students “hack” together and create an app, website, game, etc. in 24-48 hours. There will be no malicious 'hacking'.

Will hardware be available?

We do not have hardware available, but you are welcome to bring your own. Due to building fire codes, please do not bring soldering kits.

Are you sending out acceptances? Is there a deadline to apply? Is there a waitlist?

We will send out acceptances after the November 1st application deadline or when the maximum amount of hackers we can support is reached. Applications will close November 1st at 11:59 PM. There will be a waitlist the day of the event for any local hackers who want to fill the spots of any accepted hackers who do not attend.

I still have a question!

Email us at contactemoryhacks@gmail.com

#### ABOUT US

#### We are UniCS, the Computer Science Society at the esteemed University of Manchester. Our mission is to foster unity among students through a diverse array of student-led events, with our hackathons standing out as our flagship offerings, widely celebrated within our community.

#### In our pursuit of excellence, we proudly collaborate with Major League Hacking (MLH), adhering to their esteemed code of conduct. MLH, an organization that has successfully brought together a staggering 55,000 hackers, mirrors our own ambition to unite and empower the tech community.

#### Furthermore, we take pride in our past achievements, having secured victory in the 2015 autumn season, as well as triumphs in the 2018 and 2019 seasons.

#### Take a look at our past events and submissions!

#### Below are the sponsors making this event possible!

#### FAQS

#### If you have any further questions, feel free to contact us through Discord!

What is a hackathon and what can I expect from it?

A hackathon is a collaborative event where people come together to solve problems using technology. Work in teams to brainstorm, design, and realise a project over 24 hours, with each creation presented to a panel of judges. Prizes are awarded to the winning teams and mini-events / workshops run throughout. Hackathons are a great way to challenge yourself, learn new skills, and have a great time with new people!

What should I bring to the hackathon?

Please do not forget your student ID! This is crucial as we will use this to register you on the day. In addition, you could bring your laptop, charger, any hardware you might need, and any other tools or resources you would like. We also recommend bringing a change of clothes, toiletries, and anything else to stay comfortable during the event. Food and drink will be provided, but you are welcome to bring or buy your own if you prefer.

How do I register for the hackathon?

Unfortunately, the registration period for GreatUniHack 2025 has already passed.

Is registration for the event free of charge?

Yes, registration is completely free of charge! Feel free to join without any hesitation.

What is the eligibility criteria for participants?

You need to be an Undergraduate/Postgraduate at any university and 18+. We encourage all levels of ability to have a go!

How does the competition work

There will be a set of challenges or themes available for you to base your project around. Each challenge has its own set of rules, judging criteria, and expected outcome/delivery provided by the challenge setter (sponsors, societies, the organizing team, etc.)

How are teams formed?

If you already have a team, you'll be able to register with them when the form opens. If not, there will be plenty of opportunity to join a team at the beginning of the event - hackathons are about meeting new people too! All teams must be 1-4 members.

Are pre-made or previous projects allowed in the hackathon?

No, using pre-made or previously submitted projects is considered cheating according to MLH guidelines. If a team or participant is found to have used such work, we are required to report it to MLH, and it may result in disqualification of both the team and the individual involved.

What are the prizes and awards for winners?

We have an awesome line-up of prizes for the winners of various challenges. We'll reveal the prizes before the hackathon, so stay tuned!

Will food and drink be provided for participants?

We'll provide everyone with main meals, a midnight snack (covering all dietary requirements), as well as snacks and drinks throughout the event. A full menu will be available before the hackathon.

Can I participate in multiple hackathon challenges?

Of course you can! Just keep in mind that you have a limited amount of time and resources, so plan wisely!

### Website 182: MakeUC 2025
- Card URL: [https://makeuc.io/](https://makeuc.io/)
- Referenced by: MakeUC 2025
- Rendered title: MakeUC
- Final URL: [https://makeuc.io/](https://makeuc.io/)
- Expanded FAQ/accordion controls: 10

#### MakeUC 2025

November 8-9th, 2025

[View Live Site!](https://makeuc.io/live)

##### About

MakeUC is a 24-hour hackathon hosted by IEEE at the University of Cincinnati. MakeUC was first held as a fully-online hackathon in the fall of 2020. We then had our first hybrid hackathon in 2021, which was a huge success! We have continued hosting MakeUC both in person and online every fall since then, and we always do our best to improve the hacker experience from year to year. We consistently have over two dozen amazing sponsors, hundreds of registrants, tens of thousands of dollars in prizes, and plenty of fun activities.

Here, we believe that the most critical challenges must be solved by interdisciplinary and diverse teams. All you need to participate is a passion for technology and bettering the world. You will have tools, APIs, and workshops at your fingertips to help you learn and accomplish your vision. Whether you are an experienced hacker or a complete beginner, you are welcome at MakeUC. We cannot wait to see what you all create!

The MakeUC hackathon is brought to by
[IEEE@UC](https://ieee.uc.edu) .

###### Past Hackathons

[Fall 2020](https://makeuc-2020.devpost.com/) [Fall 2021](https://makeuc-2021.devpost.com/) [Fall 2022](https://makeuc-2022.devpost.com/) [Fall 2023](https://makeuc-2023.devpost.com/) [Fall 2024](https://makeuc-2024.devpost.com/)

##### Tracks

###### Green Tech

Green technology projects can be eco-friendly or implement an idea to help reduce damage to the environment.

###### Education

Education projects aim to improve the accessibility of information or upgrade students' learning experiences.

###### Social Issues

Social issue projects use technology to raise awareness and find solutions to issues such as discrimination.

###### Security

Security projects demonstrate security concerns/pitfalls, implement solutions to existing weaknesses, and more.

##### Frequently Asked Questions

###### WHAT IS A HACKATHON?

###### A hackathon is an event where student developers use their passion for technology to create a project in 24 hours. It is a chance to collaborate and create something unique such as an app, robot, or website! Hackers will think outside of the box and hopefully learn something new along the way in our engaging environment!

###### WHEN IS MAKEUC?

###### MakeUC 2025 will start on November 8th and lasts 24 hours, ending on November 9th.

###### WILL MAKEUC BE IN PERSON?

###### MakeUC 2025 will be held in person. In-person spots will be limited, if you do not recieve an in person spot please reach out to our organizers at info@makeuc.io

###### WHO CAN ATTEND?

###### Registration is open to high school students aged 16 and above, as well as individuals currently enrolled in higher education programs, with in-person participation limited to those 18 and older.

###### HOW MUCH DOES IT COST?

###### Nothing! The entire event is free for any student, with development tools and prizes all included. We will be sharing free resources from our sponsors (i.e. APIs) post-registration!

###### WHAT IF I'M NEW TO HACKATHONS?

###### Wonderful! MakeUC is open to all skill levels, beginners to veterans. There is nothing we love more than helping our hackers learn something new while they build something cool! We will have mentors available during the hackathon to answer any questions that you may have!

###### HOW DO I FORM TEAMS?

###### Teams are formed at the event (we will be having a team formation segment), but you're free to organize before the event if all participants are registered. Team size must be between 1 and 4 people.

###### WHAT IF I HAVE A SLOW INTERNET CONNECTION?

###### Plan ahead! Inform your team members about the situation and try to collaborate as much as possible. Just make sure to download all developer tools in advance!

###### WILL I BE ABLE TO MEET SPONSORS?

###### Yes! Many sponsors will be giving workshops throughout the event that all hackers can attend. We will also have an in-person sponsor expo where you can interact directly with sponsor representatives.

###### WHAT ARE HACKATHON TRACKS?

###### Tracks are themes or impact areas for projects meant to assist you with building your idea. You are not required to submit your hack to a track to win a prize!

##### Sponsors

###### Are you interested in sponsoring MakeUC? Please email us at contact@makeuc.io!

###### Adonis

[1819](https://innovation.uc.edu/)

###### Swallowtail

###### Morpho

[Kloob](https://www.kloob.app/)

#### Hack Trent

#### November, 2026

[Register Interest](https://forms.cloud.microsoft.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=3-mzllVhrE2u0ngohYEq7MU0lXDx8HRLmdCZbODkapRUNkhYQVlTNFlZU0RMR0dZQlo5S082WlJPVCQlQCN0PWcu)

About Us

HackTrent is the premier hackathon event hosted by Trent University. Join us for an exciting weekend of innovation, collaboration, and creativity. HackTrent offers an inclusive and inspiring environment to bring your ideas to life. Register now and be a part of the HackTrent experience!

The HackTrent Experience

##### Last Year We Had...

###### 21+ Projects

###### 13+ Universities & Schools

###### 240+ Applications

###### 180+ Attendees

##### Thanks for a great HackTrent 2025!

##### Thank you to our Sponsors

##### FAQ

##### What is a hackathon?

A hackathon is best described as an “invention marathon”. Anyone who has an interest in technology attends a hackathon to learn, build & share their creations over the course of a weekend in a relaxed and welcoming atmosphere. You don’t have to be a programmer and you certainly don’t have to be majoring in Computer Science.

##### Does HackTrent help with travel reimbursements?

Unfortunately, HackTrent isn’t able to provide hackers with travel reimbursements. We have in certain cases provided it in the past but we’re always on the hunt to find more sustainable ways to bring more students to hackathons.

##### MLH Code Of Conduct

For more information regarding MLH's code of conduct, please visit MLH Code of Conduct: https://github.com/MLH/mlh-policies/blob/main/code-of-conduct.md.

##### I just graduated, can I still come to an event?

Yes. Anyone who has graduated within the last 12 months is still eligible to attend an HackTrent. After that, there’s plenty of non-student hackathons that exist for you to consider attending.

##### What if I don’t know how to code?

Not a problem! The entire Major League Hacking team knew very little when they each attended their very first hackathon. It’s entirely irrelevant what your experience is going into a hackathon, it’s more about your interest in technology. Every HackTrent Member Event is passionate about making their hackathons very welcoming and beginner-friendly. If you’re wanting to learn how to code outside of a hackathon, there’s a fantastic blog post that you should read. After, you should check out the following: Treehouse, Codecademy and the Major League Hacking blog to get started.

##### Who can participate?

Students of any education level from anywhere in the world are eligible to apply for Hack Trent! All attendees must be 18 years or older to participate.

##### How much does it cost to attend Hack Trent?

Admission to Hack Trent is completely free! We will also provide food and accommodation for the entire event, as well as all the mentorship our hackers need to build something incredible.

##### What if I’ve never been to a hackathon before?

Hack Trent welcomes students of all skill levels. Many students attend Hack Trent as their first hackathon, and they always have a blast! We’ll provide mentors and workshops to help you with your project; hackathons can be a great place to learn new skills in a short amount of time. Just be eager to learn, and excited to meet lots of awesome people.

##### How many people can be on a team?

Hackers may form teams of up to 3 people. There are no restrictions for who they can be, so you can team up with hackers from any school, country, or experience level. Teams can be formed before or during the event at hackers’ own discretion.

##### Will Hack Trent 2025 happen in person or virtually?

Hack Trent 2025 will be an in-person hackathon, hosted at Trent University's Peterborough Campus in Ontario, Canada. Stay tuned to hear the latest updates by following our social media channels (@hacktrentu on Instagram and @HackTrent on LinkedIn).

##### Our Team

##### Contact Us

tcsca@trentu.ca

###### Follow and Explore More!

Website made with at Trent University

Peterborough, Ontario, Canada

We are

#### HackUTD

Largest 24 hour university hackathon in North America.

##### Our Mission

We inspire students to innovate and learn new technologies through hackathons, 24-hour events with challenges, free food & merch, and fun games & activities.

We host HackUTD, North America's largest 24 university hackathon. We also assist with other hackathons at UTD, and host helpful workshops that anyone can attend. Regardless of what we’re working on, we aim to make our hackathons accessible and open to everyone. Glad to see you here!

More about us

### Website 189: HackUMass

Pre-Register Now!

#### Why HackUMass?

HackUMass is the perfect place for students of all backgrounds and experience levels to dive into the world of technology. As a beginner-friendly hackathon, we provide hands-on workshops and dedicated mentorship to teach you the skills you need to apply technology in your field. Over the course of 36 hours, you’ll have the chance to collaborate with like-minded students from across the country, build an exciting project with our support, and compete for amazing prizes. Beyond the competition, HackUMass is also an incredible opportunity to network with industry professionals, gain real-world experience, and join a vibrant community of innovators who share your passion for learning and creating.

#### Our Sponsors

[Sponsor PITUN](https://pit-un.org/) [AWS](https://aws.amazon.com/) [Honda Research](https://www.99plabs.com/about-us) [College of Engineering](https://engineering.umass.edu/) [CICS](https://www.umass.edu/cics/) [CDSAI](https://ds.cs.umass.edu/) [CICS Makerspace](https://groups.cs.umass.edu/makerspace/) [Insomnia Cookies](https://insomniacookies.com/) [M5](https://www.umassamherstm5.org/) [Silver Screen Design](https://www.silverscreendesign.com/) [UMass Makerspace](https://sites.google.com/umass.edu/umassmakerspace/) [Analog Devices](https://www.analog.com/) [MLH](https://mlh.io/) [Pure Buttons](https://www.purebuttons.com/) [Amherst Copy](https://amherstcopy.com/) [Claude AI](https://claude.com/product/overview)

#### Why Sponsor Us

Sponsoring HackUMass offers companies a unique opportunity to connect with some of the brightest and most ambitious students in technology and engineering. As one of the largest hackathons in the Pioneer Valley, HackUMass brings together over 600 participants from top universities across the nation, all eager to innovate, learn, and showcase their skills. Sponsors gain exclusive access to a highly talented pool of future engineers and leaders through resume directories, direct networking, mentorship, and workshops. Beyond recruiting, HackUMass provides companies with a platform to highlight their technologies, inspire participants to build projects aligned with their vision, and establish their brand as a champion of innovation and education. By partnering with HackUMass, sponsors not only fuel the growth of the next generation of creators but also benefit from meaningful engagement and visibility in a dynamic, collaborative environment.

Sponsor HackUMass

#### Frequently Asked Questions

What is a hackathon?

A hackathon is a competitive sprint where people come together to collaborate and build innovative software and hardware projects to win prizes. However, students also have the option to participate in other activities instead of submitting a project.

Where is HackUMass XIV?

HackUMass XIV is an in-person hackathon on the University of Massachusetts Amherst campus, with the main venue located at the Integrative Learning Center (650 N Pleasant St, Amherst, MA 01003).

Who can attend?

HackUMass XIV is open to ALL college students, including graduate students, regardless of major!

Will my travel be reimbursed?

If you apply by the early application deadline, you have the opportunity to apply for travel reimbursement. Early applications are now closed, but we invite you to attend HackUMass if you are able to support your own travel to Amherst, MA.

Do I have to work on a project?

No, participants are welcome to attend workshops, the club/sponsor fair, and network with sponsors or other students without submitting a final project! For final project submissions, the project does NOT need to be fully finished.

Do I need experience?

We welcome students of all experience levels to participate in HackUMass! Participants will find a variety of workshops ranging from beginner-friendly to advanced, mentors, and plenty of resources to help everyone learn something new.

Should I be in a team?

We encourage participants to form a team of four (or fewer). While teams with more than four members are allowed, prizes for each member are not guaranteed beyond that size. There will be opportunities to form teams prior to the event via our event Discord server, but there will also be a team-forming event following the opening ceremony on day 1 of the event.

Can I start my project before the event?

No, participants are required to start their projects after the opening ceremony and hacking formally begins. If a project is created or edited beforehand, it will result in disqualification.

Do I have to stay for 36 hours?

No, participants are encouraged to take breaks and rest throughout the event. However, teams submitting a final project must have at least one member present during the judging process on the last day.

Is there a Code of Conduct?

We are committed to creating a safe environment for everyone at HackUMass. Therefore, all attendees are expected to follow the [MLH Code of Conduct](https://static.mlh.io/docs/mlh-code-of-conduct.pdf) and abide by the [University of Massachusetts Amherst Code of Student Conduct](https://www.umass.edu/dean_students/sites/default/files/documents/07.01.2019%20Code%20of%20Student%20Conduct.pdf) .

Will photographs and videos be taken during the hackathon?

Yes. HackUMass will take photos, audio, and video throughout the event. By participating in the event, you consent to the use of your image or voice in HackUMass materials, including on our website, social media, and other promotional or educational content. By participating in HackUMass, you are consenting to the use of your likeness and agree to release, defend, hold harmless, and indemnify HackUMass from any and all claims involving the use of your picture or likeness. If you prefer not to appear in photos or recordings, please email us at team@hackumass.com

#### YCP Hacks

##### November 6 - 8, 2026

##### York College of Pennsylvania

###### [Coming
Soon!](http://146.190.66.30:4174/)

About

###### To York and Beyond

Conveniently nestled in the middle of rural Pennsylvania, YCP Hacks is a college level hackathon with small town charm. An hour north of Baltimore, an hour and a half from Philadelphia, and 2 hours from Penn State places it right in the middle of Pennsylvania Dutch Country (presumably made of Funnel Cake). The event is set on the charming York College of Pennsylvania campus, with all the facilities, people and camaraderie that comes with it. Our mission is to promote technical knowledge, innovation, and entrepreneurship in York and beyond, and we just can't wait to see what you do next.

FAQ

###### What's a hackathon?

A 36 hour event where you work together with a team to make something awesome. You don't need to know how to code or be a supreme hacker, just how to be creative! You can also get help from our mentors and speakers who will be attending.

###### Can I come?

We'll take any college student from anywhere on earth! We sadly cannot accept students under the age of 18. All students from outside of York College may be asked to attend online if Covid protocols change

###### Where is this going to be?

The Willman Business Center of [York College!](https://goo.gl/maps/UzhJ14rStMz) There will be free parking [in the adjacent lot for the entire weekend.](https://goo.gl/maps/p1itMccEKvx)

###### When will this be?

November 7 - 9 2025

###### Why?

Learn new things, meet new people, and tons of free food and swag.

###### Will you reimburse travel?

If we get enough sponsors this season, we may offer it closer to the event date. At this time we cannot confirm it though. Apply and we'll email our applicants with more information as it becomes available.

###### I don't have a team so....

Well you're in luck! We have tons of team building activities if you don't have a team to come with you. You can also work alone if you'd like.

###### But I can't code!

We'll have tons of great workshops and mentors to help you learn and build something amazing!

###### How much does it cost?

Nothing! Registration is free and food will be provided for you. And of course, swag is free too.

###### Will there be hardware available?

Yes! Hardware will be provided.

###### What should I bring?

ID, Clothing, sleeping bag, and any necessary tech (and chargers). And deodorant. Please.

###### Do I keep my Intellectual Property?

Yes! Participants retain 100% of the intellectual property that they create during the event.

###### Are there any rules?

You can find the MLH code of conduct [here](https://static.mlh.io/docs/mlh-code-of-conduct.pdf).

###### You didn't answer my question!

Send it to us at info@ycphacks.io!.

About Section

##### Who we are

TigerHacks is a 48-hour hackathon hosted by the University of Missouri. It is a great opportunity to learn, build, and connect with other students who are passionate about technology.

- 3 days of fun!
- Welcome to any skill level
- Over $8K in prizes

FAQ

##### Frequently Asked Questions

What is a hackathon, actually?

A hackathon is an event where people work to develop a software or hardware project within a short period of time. At TigerHacks, attendees work solo or in small teams to build projects that fit our yearly theme. At the end of the event, projects are judged for the chance to win prizes!

When is TigerHacks taking place?

This year, TigerHacks is taking place November 7th-9th.

Where is TigerHacks taking place?

TigerHacks takes place at Lafferre Hall at the University of Missouri – Columbia. Our full address is: 416 S 6th St, Columbia, MO 65201.

What time does TigerHacks start and end?

Attendee check-in will begin at 4:30pm on November 7th. Opening ceremonies will start at 6:00pm. Closing ceremonies will start at 5:00pm on November 9th and should tentatively last an hour.

Who is allowed to participate in TigerHacks?

All undergraduates from a university/college are welcome to attend and participate. We've made the decision to close registration to high school students. We encourage you to participate in [MissouriHacks](https://missourihacks.org/) instead!

What should I bring to TigerHacks?

Bring your computer and charger! Also make sure to bring hygiene items like deodorant, toothpaste, and a toothbrush. And bring copies of your resume for the career fair!

What is at TigerHacks?

There'll be lots of food and swag, along with fun events and opportunities to connect with businesses!

Are there career opportunities?

Yep! We will be holding a career fair, so make sure to bring copies of your resume!

I have dietary restrictions. Will there be accommodations?

Yes! When you register, let us know and we will have food options available for you!

Will there be places to sleep?

We have 10-15 mattresses available, for those who are traveling to Mizzou!

What is the maximum and minimum team size? Who can be on the team?

Teams can have anywhere from 1 to 4 people in them. You'll still get all 4 prizes even if you have fewer than 4 members.

I'm a beginner! Can I still come?

Of course! We have a beginner category just for people like you!

What is the difference between Beginner and Developer?

The Beginner category is open to freshman/sophomore students in CS/IT and those in non-computing majors. When you register, we ask for your skill level and resume. If our team feels that your situation does not merit Beginner, we'll reach out!

Am I required to be at TigerHacks the whole time? Can I attend virtually?

You can leave the TigerHacks buildings at any time! But there is no virtual option, so you must check in on Friday in Lafferre and at least one member of your team must be present at closing ceremony if you want to collect your prizes. You must also have at least one member of your team present for judging.

Are we allowed to use ChatGPT?

If you use ChatGPT or other AI tools, make sure to declare this when submitting your project. Failing to do so will result in your disqualification.

How can I improve my score?

Make sure to take a look at the rubrics for the categories you're submitting to! Also, make sure to have a mentor for your project. Mentors are people associated with TigerHacks who help you with your project. They can be a sponsor, a volunteer, or even a member of the planning committee!

Can I submit my project to multiple categories?

Yes, but you cannot submit to both developer and beginner.

I have a question that isn't on here.

Feel free to send us a message on our [Discord](https://discord.gg/6bRrH89ZN5)!

### Website 194: Global Hack Week: API Week
- Card URL: [https://events.mlh.io/events/12815-global-hack-week-api-week](https://events.mlh.io/events/12815-global-hack-week-api-week)
- Referenced by: Global Hack Week: API Week
- Rendered title: Global Hack Week: API Week | Major League Hacking
- Final URL: [https://events.mlh.com/events/12815-global-hack-week-api-week](https://events.mlh.com/events/12815-global-hack-week-api-week)
- Expanded FAQ/accordion controls: 0

#### Global Hack Week: API Week

###### Global Hack Week (GHW) - 2026

Friday November 7, 2025

10:00AM to Nov 13, 1:00PM EDT


###### Event Description

Spend a week hacking with the MLH community at Global Hack Week: API!

Global Hack Week takes place throughout the year to give you a chance to complete technical challenges, network with the community, enjoy fun live sessions, build technical projects, and make new memories.

###### Livestreams and Mini Events

Throughout GHW, we'll have daily workshops live-streamed on our Twitch to give you the chance to pick up new skills and build something together with the community. In between workshops, we'll run mini-events in the MLH Community Discord for you to join in and meet friends as you take a break from hacking.

###### Challenges and Points

At GHW API Week we will have plenty of challenges to keep you busy. Challenges will range from social challenges urging you to connect with other members of the community, technical challenges that will expand your coding skills, and design challenges to refine your skills as a creator and artist. Some of these challenges will be completed live on our twitch stream, so you can follow along and complete it with the community. We cannot wait to see all that you learn, build, and share.

Earn experience points for yourself by completing our challenges throughout GHW. Challenges aren’t the only way to earn points. You can also receive a point each time you check in for a live session, so the more you attend, the more points you’ll rack up. They can be as simple as posting on your social media or as advanced as building a project and creating a full demo video for it. We’ll leave it to you to choose which challenges you want to take on. Feel free to work collaboratively with others on these.

###### Who can attend?

Locations have different requirements for who can attend. This location is open to the following:

Minors (Between 13-18) Working Professionals Students- University Students - Secondary/High School Students - Other

###### Links & Resources


### Website 195: SBUHacks
- Card URL: [https://hack.sbcs.io](https://hack.sbcs.io)
- Referenced by: SBUHacks
- Rendered title: SBUHacks 2026
- Final URL: [https://hack.sbcs.io/](https://hack.sbcs.io/)
- Expanded FAQ/accordion controls: 0

This website is currently under construction!We are hard at work planning SBUHacks 2026, and we can't wait to share all the exciting details with you soon. Stay tuned for updates on:

- the exact date and location of the event
- the theme and tracks
- the schedule
- the prizes
- the sponsors

The website currently serves as a placeholder with some basic information and a registration form. Check back soon for updates, and in the meantime, follow us on social media for the latest news and announcements!

If you are interested in getting involved, have any questions, or just want to say hi, feel free to reach out to us at sbcomputingsociety@cs.stonybrook.edu.

##### About

SBUHacks is Stony Brook University's largest yearly hackathon, a competition where teams of students come together and create innovative and themed projects from scratch. In previous years, we've brought together hundreds of students for hours of innovation, collaboration, and creative problem-solving.

No experience is needed— whether you're a seasoned developer or those who's going to write their first line of code, SBUHacks is your chance to build something amazing, learn new skills, and connect with fellow creators.

We'll bring the pizza, swag, and prizes; you bring your ideas and enthusiasm.

What are you waiting for?

[Register Now!](https://forms.gle/m9gXA8huQxNT5yze9)

Pictures from SBUHacks 2025

##### Terms

###### Eligibility

You are eligible if, at the time of the hackathon (October 2026), you are over the age of 18 and:

- are enrolled at any level of study at a traditional (high school, college, etc.) institution,
- are enrolled at any level of study at a nontraditional (coding boot camp, home school, online, etc.) institution,
- or have graduated from any of the above in the past year.

If you are unsure if you meet these requirements, please email us at sbcomputingsociety@cs.stonybrook.edu. Note that 80% of spots will be reserved for those who meet any of the above criteria.

Cancellation and Fees[open]

Code of Conduct[open]

Disclaimer[open]

##### Reach out to us!

Interested in sponsoring or contributing to SBUHacks 2026? We would love to hear from you!

If you have any questions, feel free to send us an email at sbcomputingsociety@cs.stonybrook.edu.

And be sure to follow our socials!

sección de Organizadores

##### Organizadores

FAQ Preguntas Frecuentes

##### Preguntas Frecuentes (FAQ)

¿Qué es un hackatón? -

Un hackatón es un evento intensivo en el que programadores, diseñadores, y otros expertos en tecnología se reúnen para colaborar en la creación de soluciones a un problema específico en un corto período de tiempo, generalmente de 24 a 48 horas.

¿Quién puede participar? -

Este hackatón está abierto a estudiantes apasionados por la tecnología y la resolución de problemas. No importa tu nivel de experiencia, ¡lo importante es la creatividad y las ganas de aprender!

¿Necesito tener un equipo para registrarme? -

No es necesario. Puedes registrarte de forma individual y unirte a un equipo antes el evento. Puedes registrarte [aqui](https://forms.gle/uMevFxTfFytwHA6L9)

¿Qué debo llevar al evento? -

Te recomendamos traer tu computadora portátil, cargador, y cualquier otro hardware con el que te sientas cómodo trabajando. También es una buena idea traer una botella de agua reutilizable y muchas ganas de colaborar.

¿Cuales son las normas del evento? -

Puedes consultar las normas de Axopunk [aqui.](https://itiz-hackaton.com.mx/assets/pdf/CodeOfConduct.pdf)

Puedes consultar las normas de MLH [aqui.](https://github.com/MLH/mlh-policies/blob/main/code-of-conduct.md)

Sección Contacto

##### ¿Necesitas ayuda?

Contáctanos para cualquier duda sobre el hackathon

📧 Contactanos

###### **You must REGISTER with the link below to participate in this Hackathon.**

###### [Click this link to register](https://events.mlh.io/events/12827-hack-coms)

###### **🦆 HACK.COMS 2025**

HACK.COMS is a 25-hour hackathon hosted by the Computing Organization for Multicultural Students (COMS) at RIT.

COMS is committed to building a supportive community that celebrates the talent of underrepresented students in computing.

A hackathon is a collaborative event where developers, designers, and tech enthusiasts come together to create a project within a limited time frame, typically ranging from a few hours to a few days. The focus is on creativity, innovation, and teamwork, and the end result is a functional project that is often shared and utilized in the community. Gather with like-minded developers ranging from beginners to enthusiasts as you work together to create something incredible in just one day!

**We'll have an incredible time this year! We've leveled up the experience with new food vendors, live performances, technical workshops, and new categories!**

Get ready for a fun-filled experience that welcomes everyone! Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting out, our hackathon is the perfect opportunity to showcase your skills, collaborate with like-minded individuals, and have a great time!

[ hackPHS 2026](https://hackphs.tech/#top)

Past Years

Princeton High School's Seventh Annual Hackathon

#### hackPHS 2026

into the skies...

hackPHS brings high school students from across New Jersey together for 24 hours of building, learning, meeting new people, and making something awesome.

Register [View 2025](https://2025.hackphs.tech/)

COMING SOON ...

About

##### What is hackPHS?

A student-run all-nighter built for high school students who want to make things, meet people, and try something ambitious in a day.

100+ students

24 hours

151 Moore St

October 3rd to October 4th

Free to attend

###### What is hackPHS?

hackPHS hosts over 100 high school students from across New Jersey to collaborate, innovate, and create in a 24-hour, student-organized hackathon based at Princeton High School. Whether you're a complete beginner or an experienced hacker, hackPHS will have something for you. hackPHS's goal is to provide a space for young and eager minds to collaborate with others, form new bonds, and create meaningful projects.

###### When and where is hackPHS?

hackPHS will be held in the cafeteria of Princeton High School, located at 151 Moore St, Princeton, NJ. Limited parking will be available around the school. The 2026 date and exact timing are still TBD, and we'll share those details once they're finalized.

###### Who pays for all of this?

hackPHS is completely free for everyone, thanks to our amazing sponsors.

###### What is a hackathon?

Hackathons are where creative and enthusiastic people come together to build awesome projects in a single all-nighter. With awesome workshops and incredible prizes, hackers will learn about new tech, build innovative solutions to real-world problems, and share their creation with the community, all in one event. If you are looking for a competition, a place to meet fellow hackers, or even just a place to learn how to code, hackathons are for you. If you want to learn more, see the [MLH Hackathon FAQ](https://mlh.io/faq).

#### Presented By

#### About Us

RowdyHacks is UTSA's annual hackathon, hosted by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) at UTSA. It's a weekend-long event where students, tech enthusiasts, and creative minds from all backgrounds come together to collaborate, innovate, and build real-world projects in 24 hours.

#### Who Can Attend?

Everyone is welcome. RowdyHacks is open to all students and beginners no matter your major, skill level, or background.

Whether you're a first-time hacker, an experienced developer, or just curious about tech, there's a place for you here.

##### About Knight Hacks

*In the not so distant future...*

The University of Central Florida's premier hackathon,***KNIGHT HACKS VIII,***emerges from the shadows...

During the 36-hour coding showdown, hackers will enlist the power of workshops, swag, tech talks, side quests, free food, games, and community to save the world!

Our friendsT.K.andLennyare at straits, and it's***your***job to settle the matter once and for all.

ES

#### HackMTY'26

36 hour long Hackathon at Tec de Monterrey, Monterrey NL.

- 81days
- 17hours
- 33minutes
- 01second

##### The largest student hackathon *in Latin America.*

What makes us awesome

We are the largest student hackathon in Mexico. Hosted by Tecnológico de Monterrey, a globally recognized university ranked among the top 200 universities worldwide.

All students welcome!

Whether it's your first hackathon or you're an experienced hacker, HackMTY is perfect for you and there's no entry fee.

Made by Tec ACM

HackMTY is built, planned and run end-to-end by Tec ACM, the student chapter of the Association for Computing Machinery at Tec de Monterrey. Make sure to follow us in our social media!

Student Chapter · Est. 2015

The 36-hour experience

36 hours of building, mentors on the floor, workshops with our sponsors, snacks at 3 am, and one big demo stage. An official MLH member event.

Registration opening soon

Location

##### Arena *Borregos.*

##### Be the first to know *when registration opens.*

### Website 207: Cal Hacks 12.0
- Card URL: [https://hackberkeley.org/event](https://hackberkeley.org/event)
- Referenced by: Cal Hacks 12.0
- Rendered title: Cal Hacks 12.0
- Final URL: [https://calhacks.io/](https://calhacks.io/)
- Expanded FAQ/accordion controls: 22

Hackathons @ Berkeley

[BECOME A HACKER](https://apply.calhacks.io/)

regular due oct 01

OCT 24 – 26 2025

[PALACE OF FINE ARTS@ SAN FRANCISCO, CA](https://maps.app.goo.gl/PrZNvqnPYYcuDCcF9)

the world's

LARGEST COLLEGIATE

HACKATHON is back & better than ever.

#### You Belong At Cal Hacks!

Whether you’ve barely ever coded in your life or have participated in twenty hackathons, Cal Hacks is here to give you a platform to build, to learn, and to create – the world is your oyster! As the world’s largest collegiate hackathon, we recognize that tech spaces need to do more to create environments where hackers of marginalized backgrounds can thrive. As long as you’re passionate about technology and looking to change the world, you belong at Cal Hacks.

The numbers that make our hackathon the largest — celebrating diversity, record-breaking participation, and a prize pool like no other.

48 hours

$200,000 in prizes

400+ projects

2,000+ hackers

Our Venue The Palace of Fine Arts

3601 Lyon St, San Francisco, CA 94123

[Get directions](https://maps.app.goo.gl/PrZNvqnPYYcuDCcF9)

#### Our Story - Twelve Years of Hacking

Year after year, we bring together the brightest minds to inspire breakthroughs. Scroll through to explore our history!

It was my first hackathon ever, and I can't imagine a better introduction.

Cal Hacks was the first time I ever shipped something end-to-end. That feeling was priceless.

I loved how diverse the projects were. Some were super practical, others just pure fun.

2023 2024 2014 2015 2016

#### Join us forDDoSki's Hack Month!

Prime yourself for the best Cal Hacks yet with a series of tech talks, sponsor workshops, and hack nights — show off what you're building, bring your friends (& meet new ones), and come immerse yourself among the best & brightest in tech!

##### 01/

###### INTRO TO HACKATHONS

Mon 9/296 - 7 PM Wheeler 20

Learn what hackathons are, how to apply, how to network with sponsors, and why they're the perfect space to learn, connect, and build something exciting!

[RSVP!](https://bit.ly/calhacks-intro-to-hackathons)

##### 02/

###### URM HACK NIGHT

Fri 10/105 - 9 PM Mudd Hall 100

Join us for a Hack Night for URMs—a fun space to build any project you like, connect with peers, and meet others heading to 12.0!

[RSVP!](https://bit.ly/calhacks-urm-hacknight)

##### 03/

###### PRE-HACKATHON SOCIAL

Wed 10/158 - 9:30 PM Blum Courtyard

Enjoy some food, meet other hackers, and the directors of Cal Hacks!

[RSVP!](https://bit.ly/calhacks-pre-hackathon-social)

##### 04/

###### ANTHROPIC WORKSHOP

Thurs 10/167 - 8 PM SOCS 126

[RSVP!](https://bit.ly/calhacks12-anthropic-workshop)

##### 05/

###### DDOSKI'S HACK NIGHT

Fri 10/176:30 - 8:30 PM Grimes 3-304

Come along to build things & make new friends at a mini-hackathon!

[RSVP!](https://partiful.com/e/ftz1cz6LDNZRchnGZZsW)

##### 06/

###### APPLOVIN WORKSHOP

Mon 10/202 - 3 PM Online

Over 1.6 billion people see an AppLovin ad each day, powered by Axon, our real-time recommendation system. Discover why we’re one of the most talked about companies, preview our challenge projects, and learn how you can win big prizes!

[RSVP!](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeheRLwZ9NM1B9PBhfFn0b2rbNnMk3vrmU_bbg0YgqntbIrzA/viewform)

##### 07/

###### GET HACKATHON READY: LEARN HOW TO NETWORK*

Tues 10/213 - 4 PM 2204 Bancroft Way

Learn how to network with employers at a hackathon by practicing core communication skills, developing an elevator pitch, and engaging in peer-to-peer practice to stand out in conversations. *Berkeley students only.

[RSVP!](https://berkeley.joinhandshake.com/edu/events/1836250)

##### 08/

###### CRASH COURSE IN FIGMA MAKE

Tues 10/217:30 - 8:30 PM Online

Join the Figma team for a workshop crash course in how to use Figma Make (Figma’s newest AI tool) to start with a design and prompt your way to a functional prototype, fast. During the session, we’ll cover how to write effective prompts, iterate on your product, and publish it as a functional, high-fidelity prototype that turns your ideas into a published website!

[RSVP!](https://figma.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_DYLWa_WfRN6dCFB48UND1Q#/registration)

##### 09/

###### FETCH.AI WORKSHOP

Wed 10/227 - 8 PM Wheeler 102

Discover how ASI:One searches and executes agents for your query dynamically.

[RSVP!](https://bit.ly/calhacks12-fetchai-workshop)

DDoSki hopes to see you there!

Meet our Speakers

[Ali GhodsiCEO & Co-Founder DatabricksAli Ghodsi is the CEO and co–founder of Databricks, responsible for the growth and international expansion of the company. In addition to his work at Databricks, Ali serves as an adjunct professor at UC Berkeley and is on the board at UC Berkeley’s RiseLab. Ali received his MBA from Mid-Sweden University in 2003 and PhD from KTH/Royal Institute of Technology in Sweden in 2006 in the area of Distributed Computing.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alighodsi/) [Dylan FieldCEO & Co-Founder FigmaDylan Field founded Figma in 2012 when he was a computer science student at Brown University. He dropped out to work full-time on Figma. Field moved to San Francisco to spend four years preparing the software for its first public release in 2016. Figma IPO'd in August 2025.](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dylanfield/)

#### FAQs

General

###### What is Cal Hacks?

###### Who can participate?

###### How many people can be on a single team?

###### What qualifies as a beginner team?

###### Do I have to have a team to participate?

###### Can I start on my project before the hackathon?

###### I can't wait until the weekend to get hacking!

###### Still have questions?

Please reach out to us at team@hackberkeley.org . We're happy to help.

Registration

###### Who can apply?

###### If I've graduated, can I volunteer to be a judge or mentor?

###### How do I apply?

###### Why are there two timelines for applying?

###### Do I get guaranteed admission if I apply early?

###### I applied! What's next?

###### Do you have a waitlist or accept walk-ins on the day-of?

Unfortunately, only hackers who were accepted and confirmed by the deadlines will be allowed in the venue. We will not make exceptions. However, we hold multiple events a year — be on the lookout for upcoming events in 2026!

In-Person Logistics

###### Where is Cal Hacks?

###### What should I bring?

###### Do I have to be in-person?

###### Will food be provided?

###### Will there be overnight accommodations?

###### Will my travel be reimbursed?

###### Will there be hardware I can use?

Yes! We’re bringing a big hardware track to Cal Hacks 12.0! There will be humanoid robots, robotic arms, 3D printers, and an array of microcontrollers and components that you can borrow for your projects. There will even be a dedicated hardware space in the venue. Come with your craziest hardware ideas!

#### DubHacks
2025

October 18-19, 2025

University of Washington, Seattle

1,100+

Participants

100+

36

Hours

$44,000

##### Play with ideas.
Build what excites you.

DubHacks 2025 takes you back to where it all began—the childhood bedroom. A space for imagination, curiosity, and bold ideas.

Now, with code instead of crayons, you get to build what makes your younger self proud. No limits, just pure creativity.

What will you create when you let yourself play?

##### Tracks

Participants will have the opportunity to create projects based on their choice of a variety of tracks:

##### Attend

I am ajudge/mentor.

As a mentor & judge, you will guide and empower DubHacks hackers by helping them navigate the cutting-edge.

Share your experienceand lend your expertise to support exploration and growth!

[Sign Up](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc5_iUEwx6cdfAM4yHGM884MGdy5SxU_oC-3EzzKgtVSsqBXA/viewform?usp=sharing&ouid=113915447387759114650)

I am aparticipant.

Whether you're new to hackathons or a seasoned participant, join us at DubHacks '25 to dream big, play around, and create the project of your dreams!

Experience36 hoursfilled with workshops, fun activities, and cool swag!

[Apply](https://docs.google.com/forms/u/2/d/e/1FAIpQLSeHpldllqmszqY9zS8TISme5Gb1opMg-MQz2OvnmVPW_8HuGQ/viewform?usp=send_form)

I am avolunteer.

As a volunteer, you will besupportinghackers, mentors, & judges over this weekend. DubHacks is made possible by volunteers like you!

[Sign Up](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScauMuRaVusECbHSwyWFcPwS7WrfFSZzrsaUC2hCAOZJW3fBQ/viewform?usp=header)

### Website 209: CodeRED: Astra
- Card URL: [https://astra2025.pages.dev/](https://astra2025.pages.dev/)
- Referenced by: CodeRED: Astra
- Rendered title: CodeRED Astra
- Final URL: [https://astra2025.pages.dev/](https://astra2025.pages.dev/)
- Expanded FAQ/accordion controls: 10

Coming this October 18-19

##### Location

Student Center South, 4810 Calhoun Rd, Houston, TX 77004

##### FAQs

Is CodeRED Astra free to attend?+

Yes! Food will be provided for the duration of the event. We will also have swag and prizes!

What kind of activities will there be?+

We will post the schedule closer to the event. There will be workshops and activities to take a break and meet other hackers and our wonderful sponsors.

Where is the event? Is it in person or virtual?+

The event is located in the Student Center South Building at the University of Houston at 4810 Calhoun Rd, Houston, TX 77004.

Who can attend? What if I don't have any experience? Do I have to be 18?+

This event is open to any students. It is beginner friendly, with workshops to help you learn during the event, and mentors available to help you as you work on your project.

Are there travel reimbursements?+

We are not able to provide travel reimbursements at this time.

What should I bring?+

Your laptop, charger, headphones, deodorant, and a pillow/blanket.

When can we start working on our project? Can I work on a previous project?+

You cannot start until after opening ceremony. You may come up with ideas, but are not allowed to start coding. You cannot work on a previous project, but can use frameworks if you clearly credit them in your readme and differentiate what you made vs what you used.

Are you sending out acceptances? Is there a deadline to apply? Is there a waitlist?+

We will send out acceptances 3 weeks before the event. If you need earlier confirmation to book travel, please reach out to our team at internal.affairs@cougarcs.com. Applications will close once we reach the maximum amount of hackers we can support, but we will open a waitlist on the day of the event for any local hackers who want to fill the spots of any accepted hackers who do not end up attending.

How do I sign up to be a mentor/judge/volunteer?+

Email us at internal.affairs@cougarcs.com!

I have a different question!+

Ask us!

This issue occurs if any requested information is missing on your MLH profile during the first attempt. If the problem persists, please contact us at hackkstate@ksu.edu .

Slide 1 Slide 2

#### What is our hackathon like?

Check out Hack K-State's 2018 closing video (or this [gallery](https://photos.app.goo.gl/Y2pYihukxk1aj6PEA) to see what Hack K-State is like. Registration for Hack K-State 2025 is currently open!

[Watch now](https://youtu.be/OBM2-lYhfdk)

Carousel Previous and Next Buttons [Previous](https://hackkstate.tech/#myCarousel) [Next](https://hackkstate.tech/#myCarousel)

Three columns of text below the carousel

MLH Logo Column 1: What's a Hackathon

##### What is a hackathon?

A hackathon is an invention and innovation competition for technology. A hackathon is a learning experience where students can come to use technology to learn new software, hardware, skills, and techniques by creating a project with a team of up to 4 people throughout the event.

[FAQ »](https://hackkstate.tech/faq)

/.col-lg-4

Hack Apple Logo Column 2: What's Hack K-State

##### What is Hack K-State?

Hack K-State is a 36-hour hackathon at Kansas State University organized by students, for students. Our organizing team works year-round to put on the event with tasks such as gathering sponsors, planning events for the weekend, and figuring out logistics with the venue, just to name a few.

[View rules »](https://hackkstate.tech/rules)

Lightbulb picture Column 3: Who can attend?

##### Who can attend?

Hack K-State is one of the few hackathons in the region that brings high school and college students together under the same event! Hack K-State is open to high school students, undergraduate college students, and graduate students from any school (in-state and out-of-state!). *Some restrictions apply, see **[rules](https://hackkstate.tech/rules)** for details*

##### Hack Cats!

This year the Hack K-State team has decided to do a rebranding on what we call our attendees! Participants can be referred to as Hack Cats as a reference to our university along with swag that is branded as Hack Cats!

/.row START THE FEATURETTES

##### Registration opens soon!
We're Official.

Registration for Hack K-State will be live in the coming weeks! The hackathon will be in-person this year October 17th-19th 2025. We are open to all high school and college students in the United States Participating in the event is 100% free and no experience is necessary.

##### Hack K-State Projects Innovate. Invent. Hack.

There is a LOT of vareity of inventions that come out of Hack K-State. Teams with varying level of experiences and backgrounds come together to make their ideas come to life. There are many free tools and hardware (like smart devices) provided during Hack K-State that the hackers can use. From software to hardware and anything in between, your imagination is the limit!

[Check out last year's projects »](https://hack-kstate-2021.devpost.com/)

##### Want to get involved?
Sponsor. Organize. Mentor.

Get involved with one of the largest combined collegiate and high school hackathon in the midwest! If you are interested in sponsoring, becoming a mentor, helping organize the event, or anything in between, please contact us at **hackkstate@ksu.edu**.

Contact Us

/END THE FEATURETTES

/.container

#### Open Source Hackfest

###### Hosted by MLH

Friday October 17, 2025

11:00AM to Oct 19, 12:00PM EDT


[Log In & Register](https://events.mlh.com/events/12537-open-source-hackfest/register)

###### Event Description

Howdy, friends!

With Hacktoberfest around the corner, we invite you to learn and explore the world of open source by creating hacks that promotes the principle of sharing knowledge for the benefit of the wider community! Come together to develop new open source hacks or build interesting hacks using just open source software! Make sure to add a [license](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/licensing-a-repository) to your project to make it truly open source!

Whether you build something on the theme or not, focus on having fun. While we'd love to have you build something on the theme, our themes are only here for inspiration. Please build whatever you'd like!

Looking for ideas to get started? Check out this [Hacker Guide](https://hackp.ac/OpenSourceHackfest-Ideas).

### Website 216: Global Hack Week: Open Source Week

#### Global Hack Week: Open Source Week

###### Global Hack Week (GHW) - 2026

Friday October 10, 2025

11:00AM to Oct 16, 2:00PM EDT


###### Event Description

Spend a week hacking with the MLH community at Global Hack Week: Open Source! Global Hack Week takes place throughout the year to give you a chance to complete technical challenges, network with the community, enjoy fun live sessions, build technical projects, and make new memories.

Spend a week hacking with the MLH community at Global Hack Week: Open Source!

Global Hack Week takes place throughout the year to give you a chance to complete technical challenges, network with the community, enjoy fun live sessions, build technical projects, and make new memories.

###### Livestreams and Mini Events

Throughout GHW, we'll have daily workshops live-streamed on our Twitch to give you the chance to pick up new skills and build something together with the community. In between workshops, we'll run mini-events in the MLH Community Discord for you to join in and meet friends as you take a break from hacking.

###### Challenges and Points

At this GHW, we will have plenty of challenges to keep you busy. Challenges will range from social challenges urging you to connect with other members of the community, technical challenges that will expand your coding skills, and design challenges to refine your skills as a creator and artist. Some of these challenges will be completed live on our Twitch stream, so you can follow along and complete it with the community. We cannot wait to see all that you learn, build, and share.

Earn experience points for yourself by completing our challenges throughout GHW. Challenges aren’t the only way to earn points. You can also receive a point each time you check in for a live session, so the more you attend, the more points you’ll rack up. They can be as simple as posting on your social media or as advanced as building a project and creating a full demo video for it. We’ll leave it to you to choose which challenges you want to take on. Feel free to work collaboratively with others on these.

###### Who can attend?

Locations have different requirements for who can attend. This location is open to the following:

Minors (Between 13-18) Working Professionals Students- University Students - Secondary/High School Students - Other

#### HACK
NC.

An annual hackathon at UNC Chapel Hill. We bring together students from across the Southeast to build, hack, and deploy in one weekend. All skill levels welcome, first-timers especially!

[REGISTER INTEREST ↗](https://hacknc.com/#host-interest)

2026

DATE

OCT 2026 TBD

WHERE

UNC CHAPEL HILL CHAPEL HILL, NC

DURATION

24 HOURS

COST

FREE

ABOUT THE EVENT § 01

500+ STUDENTS

3 DAYS

50+ PROJECTS

HackNC is a weekend for students of all skill levels to broaden their talents. Your challenge is to make an awesome project in just 24 hours. You will have access to hands-on tech workshops, sponsor networking, and exciting talks about what's happening right now in CS and tech — not to mention all of the free food, shirts, stickers, and swag!

You don't need to be a senior computer science student with four internships to show up. HackNC is explicitly built for people who are still figuring it out. The best projects usually come from people who had no idea what they were doing on Friday night.

INTEREST FORM § 02

##### ADDRESS REAL-WORLD CHALLENGES WITH NASA DATA

Whether you’re a student, professional, technologist, engineer or artist, the NASA International Space Apps Challenge offers opportunities for anyone with an interest in space and science through our hackathon and global community. All ages, skill levels, and professional backgrounds are welcome. At NASA Space Apps, there's always space for one more!

###### Real-World Challenges

The NASA Space Apps Challenge provides the opportunity to engage directly with NASA’s data, along with Space Agency Partner data. During the hackathon, teams use this data to address challenges written by NASA Subject Matter Experts, featuring topics including storytelling, software development, astrophysics, space exploration, agriculture, and more.

> ***“It is astounding to see what is possible when people come together to address the challenges we face in our endeavor to learn more about life on Earth,” said NASA Earth Science Division Director, Dr. Karen St. Germain.***

###### Meet like-minded individuals

When you participate in NASA Space Apps, you join a global community that shares a desire to learn and an interest in exploring our planet and universe through science and technology.

> ***“A team, initially uncertain about their abilities, pulled together through late night brainstorming and intense coding sessions to develop an innovative project for a real-world challenge. The pride and excitement they showed during their presentation underscored the power of collaboration and resilience in tackling complex problems.” - Obour, Egypt Local Lead***

##### 2025 NASA Space Apps Challenge Global Winners

Each year, thousands of teams submit projects that demonstrate collaboration, creativity, and address challenges we face on Earth and in space using NASA data. These projects undergo multiple rounds of judging to determine the 10 Global Winners, representing the most innovative projects submitted by participants.

[Best Use of ScienceSpaceGenes+Local Event: Berlin, Germany Challenge: Build a Space Biology Knowledge Engine](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/spacegenes/)

[Best Use of DataResonant ExoplanetsLocal Event: Mountain View, CA Challenge: A World Away: Hunting for Exoplanets with AI](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/resonant-exoplanets/)

[Art & TechnologyZumorroda-XLocal Event: Cairo, Egypt Challenge: NASA Farm Navigators: Using NASA Data Exploration in Agriculture](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/zumorroda-x/)

[Best Use of TechnologyTwistersLocal Event: Monterrey, Mexico Challenge: Will It Rain On My Parade?](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/twisters/)

[Galactic ImpactAstro Sweepers - We Catch What Space Leaves BehindLocal Event: Universal Event Challenge: Commercializing Low Earth Orbit (LEO)](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/astro-sweepers-we-catch-what-space-leaves-behind/)

[Best Mission ConceptPureFlowLocal Event: Aracaju, Brazil Challenge: Your Home in Space: The Habitat Layout Creator](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/pureflow2/)

[Most InspirationalPhotonics OdysseyLocal Event: Chennai, India Challenge: Commercializing Low Earth Orbit (LEO)](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/photonic-force/)

[Best Use of StorytellingHerCode SpaceLocal Event: Universal Event Challenge: Stellar Stories: Space Weather Through the Eyes of Earthlings](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/hercode-space/)

[Global ConnectionGaia+LEOLocal Event: Seattle, WA Challenge: Commercializing Low Earth Orbit (LEO)](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/gaialeo/)

[Local ImpactQUEÑARISLocal Event: Arequipa, Peru Challenge: BloomWatch: An Earth Observation Application for Global Flowering Phenology](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/2025/find-a-team/watana-project/)

1/4

Statistics are cumulative over the entire history of the NASA Space Apps Challenge. [Click here](https://www.spaceappschallenge.org/about/results-and-metrics/) to see event-specific results and metrics.

##### WHAT IS HACKHARVARD?

sponsors/2026.log status: pending

###### Our Sponsors

Coming Soon▮

◆Attending MHacks◆

##### Questions, answered.

01What is MHacks?+

MHacks is the University of Michigan's premier student-run hackathon, held annually in Ann Arbor. It brings together hundreds of students from across the country for a weekend of building, learning, and competing.

02Who can attend?+

MHacks is open to all currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students. Students of all skill levels and disciplines are welcome — no prior hackathon experience required.

03Where is MHacks?+

MHacks is held on the University of Michigan's campus in Ann Arbor, Michigan. Venue details will be shared with registered participants closer to the event date.

04Are there travel reimbursements?+

We offer travel reimbursements on a limited basis for participants traveling from outside the Ann Arbor area. To qualify, you must submit your application before the early deadline (Aug. 7). Apply during registration and we'll follow up with details.

05How much does MHacks cost?+

MHacks is completely free to attend. We cover meals, snacks, and event resources throughout the entire 24-hour event, thanks to our generous sponsors.

06How do teams work?+

Teams can have 1–4 members. You can form a team before the event or find teammates at our team formation session at the start of the hackathon.

07What if this is my first hackathon?+

First-timers are absolutely welcome. We'll have intro workshops, beginner-friendly resources, and dedicated mentors to help you build something great regardless of your experience level.

◆October 3–4, 2026◆

##### Come build something that grows.

Applications open June 22. Grab your spot for 24 hours of building, mentorship, and one unforgettable weekend in Ann Arbor.

[Apply Now](https://www.mhacks.org/) Sponsor Us

- Card URL: [https://andhacks.cs.wm.edu/](https://andhacks.cs.wm.edu/)

#### &Hacks 2026

Sept. 25–27

[Register](https://wmsas.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_6S8vNLg8wb4xpum) [Sponsor](https://wmsas.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_57iVqHk2QHKUzpY)

##### AI & imagination = imaginAItion

AI promotes endless avenues of creativity, project building, and integration into all sorts of academic disciplines. At &HACKS XII, hackers will use AI to its fullest potential. What can one do with AI&Linguistics, AI&Chemistry, AI& wherever your imagination will take you.

Contact

##### Reach the &Hacks team

Whether you are planning to attend, sponsor, mentor, or need accommodations before the event, send a note and the organizers will point you in the right direction.

Email **computing@wm.edu** General questions, sponsorship, and support

[Discord **Join the community server** Meet teammates, ask quick questions, and stay in the loop](https://discord.gg/ve8JwXSB) [Instagram **@andhackswm** Follow for announcements, reminders, and event updates](https://www.instagram.com/andhackswm/)

##### FAQ

What is a hackathon?

A hackathon is a free, weekend-long tech event where students learn new skills, build interesting projects, and have fun! We will have free merch, free food, and industry professionals to connect with!

Do I need coding experience?

No coding experience is necessary! In fact, our best hacks have occasionally come from teams of non-CS people.

Can I attend &hacks?

Come to &hacks! Work on a project (whether you have a team or not) to win prizes, or just come for the workshops and good vibes! No pressure; do what you feel. Please note for highschoolers that we are unable to chaperone minors.

Are there rules?

You can use AI at &hacks! More material can be found in the Major League Hacking Code of Conduct.

Can I go solo?

You can go solo or work in a team! Join our discord server to find a team: DISCORD, or attend the team-building workshop at &hacks itself.

What if I don't submit a project?

That's OK! You can still finish it on your own time after &hacks concludes.

When can I start my project?

You can start your project when the schedule comes out! If you decide to continue a previous project, please make sure to be transparent about what you coded during &hacks and what portions were completed prior to the hackathon!

What if I have more questions?

Please reach out to us with any questions or concerns at computing@wm.edu!

#### ABOUT

All About hackUMBC

Join us at UMBC for a 24-hour hackathon happening on September 26th-27thin the ITE and Engineering Buildings. Work alongside fellow students, bring your ideas to life, and develop your skills through engaging workshops!

With lots of fun activities, amazing prizes, and swag for all participants, you'll be in for an exciting day!

Curious about the team that makes hackUMBC possible?[Meet our organizing team! (Click here)](https://hackumbc.tech/team)

Prizes

#### 1st Place

TBD

#### 2nd Place

#### 3rd Place

#### SCHEDULE

hackUMBC 2026 Hackathon Schedule

7:30 AM to 8:00 AM

Event

###### TBD

10:00 AM to 11:00 AM

7:00 PM to 8:00 PM

Scroll horizontally, swipe, or hold middle mouse button and drag, to see all events

#### FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

What is hackUMBC?

hackUMBC is UMBC’s official student run hackathon, best known for hosting the university’s annual 24 hour hackathon each fall. It brings together students of all backgrounds and skill levels to learn new technologies, collaborate on creative projects, and build software or hardware solutions in a supportive, hands on environment.

Who can attend the hackathon?

Any high school or university student is welcome! You don’t need to be a UMBC student to participate, though the event will be held on campus.

How do I register?

Click the registration button on our home page and fill out the form. You’ll also have the option to submit your resume!

How do teams work for the hackathon?

You can either work solo or in teams of up to 4 members.

What can I make? May I build on an existing project?

You can make anything you want! Web, mobile, hardware, and desktop projects are all encouraged and welcome! However, we strongly discourage building upon existing projects, as they will not be eligible for prizes.

Will there be FREE FOOD provided?

Yes, and we accommodate all dietary restrictions, including vegan, vegetarian, halal, gluten free, and common allergies. Just let us know on the registration form!

Do I have to be an expert programmer, or know how to code?

Not at all! hackUMBC is a great way to learn something new! Workshops for beginners will be readily available to all participants!

What if I have other obligations during the hackathon?

We understand that it may be challenging to clear up the whole weekend, so you are allowed to come and go as needed!

Is travel reimbursement provided?

Unfortunately, we aren’t able to offer travel reimbursements this year due to budget limitations. We’d still love to have you join us at hackUMBC, and we’ll make sure the weekend is worth the trip!

What if I would like to help out with the hackathon?

You can fill out a volunteer interest form that will be released a few weeks before hackUMBC begins. We will also begin looking for new organizers in the spring!

Is there anywhere I can see some examples of old contestants’ projects?

Yes! You can see examples at: [https://hackumbc-2025.devpost.com/project-gallery](https://hackumbc-2025.devpost.com/project-gallery)

Where can I view the MLH Code of Conduct?

You can view it here: [MLH Code of Conduct](https://github.com/MLH/mlh-policies/blob/main/code-of-conduct.md)

#### SPONSORS

Thank You to Our Incredible Sponsors!

hackUMBC wouldn't be possible without the generous support of our sponsors, click on their logos to check them out!

#### what is sunhacks?

sunhacks is a yearly hackathon (ideathon, coding marathon, prototyping marathon, etc) designed to support students in their innovative journeys. sunhacks is for students of all skill levels, and our job as organizers is to support you by providing the resources you need to achieve your development dreams. We provide you with workshops, mentors, community connections, and peers who are motivated to help each other succeed and reach their goals.

##### OwlHacks

September 27 - 28, 2025 | In Person | Temple University | 1925 N. 12th St., Philadelphia, Pa 19122

###### 0DAYS0HOURS0MINUTES0SECONDS

(Psst... OwlHacks is happening now)

##### Our Mission

OwlHacks is Temple University's annual, student-organized hackathon. First hosted in 2019, OwlHacks received outstanding projects, turnout, and support from our sponsors and university.

##### Logistics

event schedule

tracks

###### Saturday, September 27th

8:00 AM

Check in and Breakfast

Check in, grab some breakfast, and get ready for a day of hacking!

9:00 AM

Opening Ceremony

Welcome to OwlHacks 2025! Join us for the opening ceremony to kick off the event.

📍 Room 110AB

10:00 AM

Intro GitHub Copilot Workshop hosted by MLH

Learn how to use GitHub Copilot to help you with your projects.

📍 Room 108A

Start Hacking + OwlHacks Project Help Booth Open

Need help with your project? Stop by the OwlHacks Project Help Booth to get assistance.

10:30 AM

Intro to Google AI Studio Workshop hosted by MLH

Learn how to use Google AI Studio to help you with your projects.

11:00 AM

Secure Your Code: Hands-On Static Analysis Workshop

Join Derek Fisher, for a one-hour, instructor-led workshop on integrating and running open-source static analysis tools. Participants will clone a GitHub repo, install a lightweight SAST tool, execute scans, and interpret findings. By the end of the session, you'll have hands-on experience automating code security checks and the confidence to replicate the workflow in your own projects.

[Derek Fisher, CISSP, CSSLP, Director of Temple University's Cyber Defense and Information Assurance Program](https://www.linkedin.com/in/derek-fisher-sec-arch/)

12:00 PM

Lunch sponsored by SAP America, Inc.

Enjoy some lunch and take a break from hacking.

📍 Lobby

1:00 PM

Web Jam Starter Pack Workshop

Join David Loder, for a front-end focused session on getting a basic website up and running fast. You'll start with a simple template, customize it with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and learn tips for adding interactivity and making it your own — perfect for hackathon participants who want to build a polished site quickly.

[David Loder, President of TUDev and Web Developer for Temple's ACM chapter](https://www.linkedin.com/in/daveloder/)

2:00 PM

Mini Club + Donor Fair. Get Involved!

Come meet the Temple tech community! Clubs that have endorsed the hackathon and donors. They will be sharing what they do, how you can get involved, and the opportunities they offer to students. A great chance to network, discover new communities, and pick up some cool swag.

3:00 PM

Power Up Your Backend with Python

Join Byron Jenkins, Senior Software Engineer, Adjunct Instructor, and Content Creator, for a beginner-friendly backend workshop using Python. Learn how to add server-side functionality to your hackathon project and explore what goes into powering your app beyond the front end. Specific tools and frameworks will be announced closer to the event.

[Byron Jenkins, Senior Software Engineer @ Capital One & Adjunct Instructor @ Temple University](https://www.linkedin.com/in/byron-jenkins/)

4:00 PM

Social Event Sponsored by HCI Lab

Type Racing Tournament — go head-to-head in a fast-paced typing showdown! Advance through rounds, race fellow hackers for the top spot, and win prizes for the fastest finishers.

📍 To be Announced

5:00 PM

Don't Wait, Deploy Now! Deploying Frontend Apps

Join Prof. Ian Applebaum, for a practical session on taking your frontend app live. Learn how to prepare your project for deployment, use modern hosting platforms, and avoid common pitfalls so you can ship your hackathon project with confidence and speed.

[Ian Applebaum, Computer Science Instructor and HCI Lab researcher at Temple University](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ian-applebaum-82b824a0/)

6:00 PM

Dinner sponsored by Blackstone Launchpad

Enjoy some dinner and take a break from hacking.

09:00 PM

Bob Ross Painting Social Event Presented by MLH

Paint like Bob Ross but in an ancient version of Windows Paint while watching Bob Ross on Twitch. It’s funny AND impressive!

12:00 AM

Midnight Movie/Game Break Sponsored by HCI Lab

Hmmmmm.....

###### Sunday, September 28th

09:00 AM

Breakfast + Soft Deadline

10:00 AM

Project Submission Hard Deadline

Submit on Devpost

10:15 AM

Career Panel Sponsored by HCI Lab

Don't miss this chance to ask engineers from NVIDIA, Microsoft, Capital One, and AWS about careers, growth, and breaking into the industry.

📍 Room 116

11:00 AM

Sponsors Booth Open

Visit the sponsors booths to learn more about their companies and opportunities.

12:00 PM

Lunch Sponsored by EZ Cater

Lunch will be served

1:00 PM

Judging Begins..

Judging will begin

02:30 PM

Closing Ceremony

Join us for the closing ceremony to celebrate the end of OwlHacks 2025 and announce the winners of the hackathon.

📍 Room 110AB

04:00 PM

That's all, folks!

Thank you so much for participating and we hope to see you again next year!
